Error Annotation: Build fails at apt-get update due to Debian repository 404 / missing Release files (EOL repo).
Rationale: Otherwise structurally close to the human approach (PHP+Apache). The Git source is valid (repo exists). The failure is an environment/repo issue fixable locally without major post-fix drift.

Error Annotation: Build fails at apt-get update because Debian buster repositories return 404 / missing Release files (EOL repo).
Rationale: High divergence from human: different base image (Debian instead of PHP-Apache), different runtime model, and dependency strategy. Even after fixing repos, the stack remains substantially misaligned (architecture drift).

Error Annotation: Build fails at apt-get update due to Debian stretch repository 404 / missing Release/Packages (EOL repo).
Rationale: Some alignment with human (PHP 5.6 + Apache), but still diverges by relying on a local ZIP COPY and failing on repo setup. The alternative SourceForge download link does not show a direct 404 in our check but redirects to a generic listing page, so external retrieval is not cleanly verified.

Error Annotation: Build fails when extracting PhpCollab: unzip is missing (not installed). Also, the referenced GitHub release asset URL is invalid (HTTP 404).
Rationale: Diverges from the human reference in both dependency provisioning (missing unzip) and artifact sourcing (non-canonical URL that does not exist). This is more than a single local typo.

Error Annotation: Build fails because COPY phpcollab/ /var/www/html/ references a path not present in the build context (file does not exist).
Rationale: Moderate divergence: assumes a local source bundle instead of fetching like the human Dockerfile. Fix requires changing artifact acquisition strategy or supplying additional build context (not a single-line repo tweak).

Error Annotation: No functional errors: build succeeds and container starts (only Apache ServerName warning). Artifact download is reachable during build.
Rationale: Build+run succeed; despite a different artifact source than human, URLs are operational (validated implicitly by successful build). No divergence-induced failure.

Error Annotation: Build fails during apt-get update due to Debian buster repositories being unreachable / missing Release files (exit code 100).
Rationale: Strong divergence from the human reference: it switches to building OpenProject from source on a generic Ruby base image (different pipeline, dependency model, and runtime). Even if the repo issue were fixed, the generated workflow remains substantially misaligned.

Error Annotation: Build fails when extracting the OpenProject archive: tar reports “stdin: not in gzip format”, indicating the downloaded artifact is not a valid .tar.gz.
Rationale: Diverges from the human reference by replacing the official image-based approach with a custom “download+extract+build” pipeline. The failure suggests a broken artifact retrieval/format assumption, and the overall pipeline remains far from the human reference even after a minimal fix.

Error Annotation: No functional errors: compose starts and the container is created/attached (only a non-blocking warning about obsolete version in compose).
Rationale: Operational outcome with no failure. The warning is non-fatal and does not indicate semantic divergence affecting execution.

Error Annotation: Build fails during apt-get update due to Debian buster repositories being unreachable / missing Release files (exit code 100). Also, the Dockerfile relies on external OpenProject repository URLs that return HTTP 404 (packager repo + Release key).
Rationale: Severe divergence from the human approach: instead of using the official OpenProject image, it attempts a repo-based installation path that is currently broken (404 endpoints) and structurally different from the reference. The failure is not a single local patch aligned with the human Dockerfile.

Error Annotation: No functional errors observed: the compose stack starts and services come up (runtime logs present, DB ready).
Rationale: Although it introduces a multi-service setup (divergent from the human single-image workflow), it is operational and does not exhibit a divergence-induced failure for build/run.

Error Annotation: No functional errors observed: the compose stack starts and services come up (runtime logs present, DB ready).
Rationale: Although it introduces a multi-service setup (divergent from the human single-image workflow), it is operational and does not exhibit a divergence-induced failure for build/run.

Error Annotation: The build fails because the Dockerfile attempts to copy grafana.ini from the build context, but the file is not present.
Rationale: This introduces a structural divergence from the human Dockerfile, which does not rely on external configuration files. Fixing the issue requires removing or rethinking the configuration approach rather than a purely local patch.

Error Annotation: The build fails due to a missing grafana.ini file referenced in a COPY instruction that is not part of the build context.
Rationale: As with Bing, the Dockerfile diverges from the human reference by assuming the presence of external configuration artifacts. This represents a non-local divergence in configuration strategy.

Error Annotation: The image builds successfully but does not start the Grafana server because no CMD or ENTRYPOINT is specified.
Rationale: The Dockerfile remains close to the human reference in base image selection but omits a required execution instruction. The fix is local, but the container is non-functional as generated.

Error Annotation: No functional error. The Dockerfile matches the human reference by reusing the official Grafana image and starting the server correctly.
Rationale: The structure, base image, and execution strategy are identical to the human Dockerfile. No divergence is observed.

Error Annotation: The build fails during apt-get update due to Debian stretch repositories returning HTTP 404 (end-of-life distribution).
Rationale: Unlike the human reference, this Dockerfile diverges by abandoning the official Grafana image and attempting to reconstruct the environment from a deprecated Debian base.

Error Annotation: The build fails while downloading Grafana due to a SHA256 checksum mismatch on the fetched archive.
Rationale: The Dockerfile significantly diverges from the human reference by re-implementing Grafana installation from source and external binaries. Commands seems to be correct for building, but in this case it was better to opt for an original image

Error Annotation: The build fails during the osTicket installation step with a non-zero exit code after unpacking the release archive.
Rationale: The Dockerfile uses the same base image and the same canonical osTicket release URL as the human reference. The overall installation pipeline is equivalent, and the error does not introduce additional divergence beyond a local build failure.

Error Annotation: The build fails while using a source-archive installation (.tar.gz) of osTicket instead of the release zip used in the human Dockerfile.
Rationale: Although the referenced source archive is valid, the Dockerfile diverges from the human reference in the packaging and installation strategy (tarball vs release zip). Fixing the error requires adapting multiple steps rather than applying a single local patch.

Error Annotation: The build fails during the osTicket download step that relies on a parameterized GitHub release URL and subsequent PHP extension compilation errors.
Rationale: The Dockerfile diverges from the human reference both in how the release artifact is retrieved and in the PHP extension build pipeline. However, it requires few steps to fix it

Error Annotation: The build fails during the osTicket extraction and setup step due to a non-zero exit code after the download and unzip phase.
Rationale: The Dockerfile closely follows the human reference: same base image, same canonical osTicket release URL, and same zip-based installation strategy. The failure occurs in a local build step without introducing further structural divergence.

Error Annotation: The build fails while compiling PHP extensions (intl / mbstring) during image construction.
Rationale: The Dockerfile departs from the human reference by rebuilding and reconfiguring PHP extensions instead of relying on the base image defaults.

Error Annotation: The build fails during PHP extension configuration due to unsupported options passed to docker-php-ext-configure (e.g., GD flags).
Rationale: The Dockerfile significantly diverges from the human reference by cloning the osTicket repository and manually compiling multiple PHP extensions.

Error Annotation: Build fails immediately because rm index.html errors out (No such file or directory), stopping the pipeline at step 3.
Rationale: The overall structure is close to human (php-apache flow). The failure is a single local command robustness issue (rm should be rm -f or omitted). URL differs from human but was not exercised due to early failure; automated fetch check is inconclusive (tool reports a 400-style fetch failure rather than 404).

Error Annotation: Build fails because apt-get install requests non-available PHP packages (php7.4-fpm, php7.4-mysql, php7.4-mbstring) on the selected base image. Also, the Bludit artifact URL differs from human and returns HTTP 404.
Rationale: Severe divergence from the human reference: different stack (nginx + php-fpm), incompatible dependency assumptions, and an invalid external artifact source (404). Fix is not a single local adjustment that preserves alignment.

Error Annotation: Build completes successfully and Apache starts (only non-fatal ServerName warning).
Rationale: Matches the human reference pipeline closely and uses the same canonical GitHub archive URL as human; no divergence-induced failure is observed.

Error Annotation: Build completes successfully and Apache starts (only non-fatal ServerName warning).
Rationale: Structurally aligned with the human reference and uses the same Bludit archive URL as human; no divergence-induced failure is observed.

Error Annotation: Build fails because COPY bludit-3.9.2.zip references a file missing from the build context (file does not exist).
Rationale: Diverges from human by assuming a local artifact instead of downloading the canonical archive. Fix requires changing the acquisition strategy (or providing extra context), not just a trivial one-liner within the same pipeline.

Error Annotation: Build fails because it runs chmod on /var/www/html/bl-content, but the directory is missing after extraction/move (No such file or directory).
Rationale: The failure indicates artifact/layout mismatch relative to the human pipeline. The Dockerfile is “close” in intent but diverges in the retrieval/extraction path (different archive source), causing post-extraction structure to differ. Likely fix is to switch to the canonical human URL or adjust extraction/move logic.
